finding.summary: Stereo correlation is -0.25 with side energy 14 dB above mid energy (side -14 dB, mid -28 dB), indicating an aggressively widened image that will likely collapse poorly in mono. The single enabled FX is a chorus with no parameter values reported, and a chorus on a track named 'Wide Synth' is the most plausible driver of this decorrelation.
finding.probable_cause: The enabled VST3: Generic Chorus (fx_chain[0]) is modulating/detuning L and R to produce the extreme side-to-mid imbalance (audio.stereo.side_rms_db vs audio.stereo.mid_rms_db) and the negative correlation (audio.stereo.correlation). With no chorus parameters available to dial back, the only reversible test from this payload is a bypass preview.
finding.confidence_reason: audio.silence_fraction is low (0.05), so the level statistics are not diluted by silence, and audio.capture.isolation_verified is true. However, the track is named 'Wide Synth', so extreme width may be partially intentional; we cannot separate artistic intent from a genuine phase risk without a before/after comparison.

evidence_refs.description: Negative correlation (-0.25) places the image in the phase-caution zone.
Side RMS at -14 dB, well above mid.
Mid RMS at -28 dB, 14 dB below side.
Only active FX is a chorus, a known stereo-widening processor.
Track name suggests width is desired, limiting confidence.

proposal.reason: Preview bypassing VST3: Generic Chorus; this does not remove or delete the plugin; to test whether stereo_correlation increases.
